result.summary: Adobe exposes multiple MCP servers for creative, marketing, journey, express, Photoshop, and Acrobat workflows, plus documented HTTP APIs such as Cloud Manager, Data Collection, Journey Optimizer, and Events, and an npm-distributed `aio` CLI.
result.description: Adobe Developer provides access to APIs, events, and tooling across Adobe products including Experience Cloud, Creative Cloud, Document Cloud, and App Builder. It also publishes MCP connectors that let AI clients access Adobe capabilities such as creativity, marketing, journey optimization, express, Photoshop, and Acrobat.

adobe_oauth_s2s.label: Adobe OAuth Server-to-Server credential
adobe_oauth_s2s.setup: In [Adobe Developer Console](https://developer.adobe.com/console/), create or open a project, add the target API/service, and choose **OAuth Server-to-Server** when adding credentials. Follow the [server-to-server authentication guide](https://www.adobe.io/developer-console/docs/guides/authentication/ServerToServerAuthentication/) and [implementation guide](https://www.adobe.io/developer-console/docs/guides/authentication/ServerToServerAuthentication/implementation) to mint access tokens for the APIs your project is authorized for.

adobe_oauth_user.label: Adobe OAuth user authentication credential
adobe_oauth_user.setup: In [Adobe Developer Console](https://developer.adobe.com/console/), create or open a project, add the target API/service, and choose an OAuth user credential type such as **OAuth Web App**, **OAuth Single Page App**, or **OAuth Native App**. Configure redirect URIs and scopes as described in the [user authentication guide](https://www.adobe.io/developer-console/docs/guides/authentication/UserAuthentication/) and [implementation guide](https://www.adobe.io/developer-console/docs/guides/authentication/UserAuthentication/implementation).
